matched.reason: Both describe Errtu's goal to possess the crystal shard, introduced in the prelude.
Both refer to the united barbarian tribes attacking Ten-Towns, introduced in chapter 2.
Both express Bruenor's desire to reclaim Mithril Hall, introduced in chapter 5.
Both reveal the sentient crystal shard's manipulative nature, introduced in chapter 4.
Both describe Bruenor forging a legendary warhammer, introduced in chapter 11.
Both establish that Drizzt will train Wulfgar, introduced in chapter 12.
Both involve Heafstaag's survival after the battle, setting up his return as a future threat.
Both describe Regis entering Cryshal-Tirith as an emissary to use the pendant on Kessell, in chapter 27.
Both are about the companions' quest to find Mithril Hall, introduced in chapter 30.
Both promise that Regis's past with the thieves' guild will catch up to him, introduced in chapter 2.
Both highlight the disunity of Ten-Towns preventing an effective defense, introduced in chapter 19.
Both concern Drizzt acquiring a magical scimitar from the dragon's hoard, in chapter 22.

unmatchedGold.promise_text: Ten-Towns is fractured by internal rivalries, making a unified defense against external threats difficult.
Wulfgar, a barbarian indentured to Bruenor, nears the end of his service, harboring a blood-debt and great potential.
Akar Kessell is assembling a vast multi-tribe monster army to conquer Icewind Dale.
The demon Errtu enters the material plane seeking Crenshinibon and becomes Kessell's general.
Wulfgar receives the magical hammer Aegis-fang and swears a conditional oath not to raise it against Ten-Towns.
Errtu secretly plots to betray Kessell and claim the crystal shard for itself.
Heafstaag is magically coerced into serving Kessell, fostering deep resentment.
Wulfgar departs on a secret personal quest, implied to involve a dragon.
The dwarves of Mithril Hall survive the initial clash and will fight again.
Drizzt summons Errtu using its true name to force it to reveal Cryshal-Tirith's weakness.
Errtu vows a century-long revenge after Drizzt banishes it back to the Abyss.
Wulfgar, now king, rallies the barbarian tribes to fight against Kessell's army.
The dwarves prepare a surprise tunnel assault from beneath the enemy encampment.
Regis tricks Kessell into believing there are traitors in his ranks, aiming to sow chaos.
The assassin Artemis Entreri, sent by Pasha Pook, is hunting Regis.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: The wizards Eldeluc and Dendybar will discard or eliminate Akar Kessell after using him to kill Morkai.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: He understood now that he had been no more than a pawn in their devious designs to rid themselves of Morkai the Red.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Regis's ruby pendant has persuasive magical power.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: But it is there - I see it clearly and know the legend of such a stone.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Ten-seconds passed and Kemp had not blinked.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Drizzt can summon a powerful panther ally named Guenhwyvar from the onyx figurine.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor asks after Guenhwyvar and Drizzt produces the onyx figurine, explaining the panther needs rest.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: the great panther was beside him.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Kessell vows never to be a pawn again and will seek power and respect.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: vowing he will never again be a stooge
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: I am Akar Kessell, Tyrant of Icewind Dale!

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Akar Kessell will gather an army and become a tyrant threatening Icewind Dale.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Kessell decides he needs a castle and, guided unknowingly by Crenshinibon, discovers a duplicate crystal and intuitively understands his role in using it.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Kessell's army swept around Lac Dinneshere and crashed into Caer-Konig.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Wulfgar intends to undertake a dangerous personal mission related to his tribal heritage.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: "Of adventures and an unfulfilled vow," Wulfgar said mysteriously.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: slayer of the frost giant chieftain, Biggrin; and dragon's bane!

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu will betray Kessell and attempt to take the crystal shard for himself.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Errtu accepts with a wicked bow.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: A band of verbeeg giants hidden in a lair near Ten-Towns will be discovered and confronted by the heroes.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: A band of verbeeg led by a frost giant slips into a secret lair near Ten-Towns under cover of a violent storm, carrying weapons and supplies for Akar Kessell's army.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: the monster suddenly staggered and fell dead.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Drizzt will attempt to assassinate Akar Kessell.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: unnerved by the wizard's power over normally independent tribes, resolves to attempt to kill him before retreating to rest.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: I am Drizzt Do'Urden, and I have come to kill you.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Kessell can communicate and observe through magic mirrors, posing a surveillance threat.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: A gaunt mage's face appears in the mirror demanding Biggrin's presence.
